growth_records.cite: Wiles et al. Host Gut Motility Promotes Competitive Exclusion within a Model Intestinal Microbiota. PLoS Biology (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.1002517. [PMC4961409]
growth_records.snippet: Mono-associated Aeromonas have a growth rate shortly after collapse of 0.74 ± 0.1 hr-1 (mean ± SD, n = 5 collapses)
growth_records.notes: In vivo intrinsic growth rate of Aeromonas veronii immediately following a stochastic population 'collapse' event during mono-association in the zebrafish gut, from LSFM imaging (n=5 collapse events). Not a defined culture medium; reported directly by authors. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen, medium]

growth_records.agit: shaking (rpm not specified) for in vitro cultures; not applicable in vivo
growth_records.cite: Wiles et al. Host Gut Motility Promotes Competitive Exclusion within a Model Intestinal Microbiota. PLoS Biology (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.1002517. [PMC4961409]
growth_records.snippet: whereas Vibrio-challenged Aeromonas have 0.64 ± 0.2 hr-1 (n = 4 collapses)
growth_records.notes: In vivo intrinsic growth rate of Aeromonas veronii immediately following a stochastic population 'collapse' event while co-colonized with Vibrio ZWU0020 in the zebrafish gut, from LSFM imaging (n=4 collapse events). Reported directly by authors. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen, medium]
